Defendant'S Motion For Assignment Order, Turnover Order And Restraining Order
Off Calendar Oct-24-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Greg Groeneveid Esq., (415-308-3637) for Thomas Harris, Defendant and moving party.
Steven Miyake Esq., (415-421-4400) for Regina Thomson, Plaintiff and opposing party.

Argued. The Court adopts it's tentative ruling as follows: Defendant'S Motion For Assignment Order, Turnover Order And Restraining Order is Granted, in part, and denied, in part. Defendant's motion for a restraining order is granted. (CCP 708.520.) Plaintiff is hereby restrained from assigning or otherwise disposing of the right to payment as to the amount of $ 70,915.24 of the judgment issued in favor of Plaintiff in Thomson v. Canyon (Alameda County Superior Court case no. RG08400707.) To the extent that Plaintiff's counsel may be entitled to any of amount subject to the restrained order pursuant to his attorney lien, Plaintiff may file and serve a motion to modify or vacate. (See CCP 708.520(c).) All other requests for relief are denied. Court signs order in open Court. Judge: Ronald E. Quidachay, Clerk: Mars Goodman, Not reported (D501)

Greg Wood of Methven and Associates, 510-649-4019, appearing for defendant and moving party

No other appearances on this matter. Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: DEFENDANT THOMAS HARRIS' MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT is denied. MINI MINUTES:
NO APPEARANCE. TENTATIVE RULING ADOPTED. Defendant Thomas Harris's motion to compel further responses to form interrogatories and request for production of documents is granted.
Form interrogatories
2.3, 2.4, 17.1 (regarding requests for admissions 1, 2, 5, 7): granted. The objections are overruled and stricken. Plaintiff shall serve further verified responses within 7 days of notice of this order.
Request for production
7: granted. If all responsive documents have been produced that representation shall be verified.
8: granted. If no documents exist, any response shall comply with CCP Section 2031.220.
All requests for monetary sanctions are denied. COMMISSIONER: BRUCE E. CHAN.
